name: Issues with responses using structured outputs on the API

incident_updates.body: We have identified and resolved an issue which resulted in issues with model responses when making use of structured outputs via the Anthropic API between 16:10 UTC on April 22 and 01:59 UTC on April 23. This did not impact any requests not using structured outputs, as detailed here: https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/structured-outputs.

name: Dispatch sessions not responding in Claude Desktop

incident_updates.body: The issue affecting Dispatch has been resolved. We fixed an underlying issue that prevent the agent from sending responses to user messages. Please update to Claude desktop app 1.1.9493. We apologize for any inconvenience caused.

We've identified an issue in the latest Claude Desktop release (Claude 1.1.9310) where Dispatch sessions stop responding. Messages sent to Dispatch are received and processed, but replies do not appear in the conversation. We have identified the cause and are working on a fix. As a workaround, you can continue using standard Cowork sessions, which are unaffected.

On March 26–27, 2026, customers experienced elevated error rates when using Claude Opus 4.6 and Claude Sonnet 4.6. The issue was caused by a networking performance degradation within our infrastructure that disrupted communication between components of our serving stack. We resolved the incident by migrating the affected workloads to healthy infrastructure, restoring normal service by 9:30 AM PT on March 27.
This incident has been mitigated as of 9:30 PT / 16:30 UTC.
As of 6:40 PT / 13:40 UTC our error rate has returned to the baseline. We are still monitoring the current situation.

We have mitigated the main issue and the error rate for Opus 4.6 is hovering around 0.4% which is still high, but much better than the performance during the previous hours. We are waiting for a more permanent fix to land in the near future.
As of 5:00 PT / 12:00 UTC Sonnet 4.6 is now healthy. We are still working to fix Opus 4.6.
Sonnet 4.6 is also showing elevated errors.

name: Some users are experiencing delays in purchasing additional API credits

incident_updates.body:
During the outage, all of the following were delayed:
- customer balances
- billing for standard API usage
- issuing credit auto-recharge invoices
- access shutoff/restore for $0 in credits, and for reaching spend limits

The outage has now been resolved. There is no longer a system delay for any of the above.

name: Elevated errors for requests to Claude 3.5 Sonnet

incident_updates.body: This incident has been resolved.

Primarily from 12:38–12:51 UTC, requests to claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022 experienced elevated errors. Since this time, success rates have returned to normal. Free Claude.ai usage has been routed to Haiku, and will be returned to Sonnet 3.5 as soon as possible.
A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.
The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.

Between 21:00 UTC on October 28 through 04:15 UTC on October 30, API calls invoking tool use intermittently failed to include all parameters, including required parameters. This issue primarily affected requests to claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022 and claude-3-haiku-20240307, and has now been resolved.
The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.

We have identified broader impact to requests invoking tool use, which is resulting in some arguments failing to include some arguments, even when required. We are actively working to resolve this issue, and will provide additional updates soon.
We are continuing to investigate intermittent failures to invoke tools in the computer use beta.
We are currently investigating an issue resulting in incorrect tool calls when using the computer use beta. We will provide updates as soon as possible.
From 11:03–16:37 UTC, some users experienced elevated errors on requests to Claude 3.5 Sonnet. This issue is now resolved.
A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.
We are currently investigating this issue.
This incident has been resolved.
We are continuing to investigate this issue.
These models are currently not available
This incident has been resolved. We will return free Claude.ai usage to 3.5 Sonnet as soon as possible.

Starting from 06:00 UTC, we've seen intermittent periods of elevated errors on requests to Claude 3.5 Sonnet. As of 16:02 UTC, these issues are resolved. At this time, we have temporarily routed free Claude.ai usage to Haiku.
A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.

During this period, some users of claude.ai may have experienced inconsistencies in Claude's responses. This could have resulted in replies that did not fully align with the ongoing conversation. The issue did not involve any prompt leakage or compromise of user data. The bug has been identified and fixed, and normal service has been restored.

name: Some users saw elevated rates of 529s

incident_updates.body: The issue has been resolved.

From 19:12 UTC to 19:29 UTC, requests to the `claude-v1.0` model were issuing 529s. All other models were serving requests at the time, including requests to `claude-v1`, which automatically points to the newest Claude version.
From 8:58:47 UTC to 8:59:01 UTC, we had a partial outage that returned 500s for some set of requests. The outage has since resolved.
From 10:17 UTC to 10:18 UTC, we had a partial outage that returned 500s for some set of requests. The outage has since resolved.
From 19:04 UTC to 19:06 UTC, our models were temporarily offline. Users who issued API queries likely received back 404s and their requests were not completed.
We have fixed the issue that has impacted a majority of API traffic for one hour.
We have fixed the issue that has impacted a majority of API traffic for 30 minutes.
